title: Cheilosoria chusana
intro: Cheilosoria chusana, also known as the Himalayan lip fern, is a rare and endangered species of plant in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Cheilosoria chusana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cheilosoria chusana is often used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and as a general tonic to boost immunity.
taxonomy: Cheilosoria chusana is a species of fern in the family Cheilosoriaceae. It belongs to the genus Cheilosoria, which is a small group of epiphytic ferns. The species is native to tropical Asia and is typically found growing on trees in humid environments. Cheilosoria chusana is classified as an epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other plants for support rather than in the ground. The classification of Cheilosoria chusana is part of the broader family of vascular plants and the division of flowering and non-flowering plants.
